name: Social Events API
description: REST API for discovering, hosting, and attending social events—organizations, ticket tiers, registrations, comments, in-app notifications, OAuth/JWT auth, and Celery-backed async work. Deployed on AWS EC2 with RDS PostgreSQL and Upstash Redis.

problemStatement.problemTitle: Fragmented event management for communities and organizers
problemStatement.problemDescription: Community groups, venues, and independent organizers often rely on spreadsheets, generic form tools, and disconnected chat apps to publish events, sell or assign tickets, and keep attendees informed. That leads to double bookings, weak access control, no audit trail, and poor engagement after signup.
problemList: No single API for organizations to own events, members, and branding
Manual registration lists without capacity, waitlist, or check-in flows
Comments and notifications scattered outside the event domain
Authentication friction across web and mobile clients
Difficulty scaling traffic without caching and background email delivery

solution.solutionTitle: One API for the full event lifecycle

solutionList.title: Multi-tenant organizations
solutionList.description: Organizations with memberships, invites, join/leave, and org-scoped event listings for clubs and venues.

solutionList.title: Rich event domain
solutionList.description: Draft/publish/cancel, categories, tags, recurrence, staff roles (host, co-host, moderator), favorites, and personalized feeds.

solutionList.title: Registrations & ticketing
solutionList.description: Ticket tiers with pricing and capacity; registrations with pending, confirmed, waitlist, cancelled, attended states and host check-in.

solutionList.title: Engagement layer
solutionList.description: Threaded comments with likes and host pin/unpin; in-app notifications with read state and email templates.

solutionList.title: Cloud-ready operations
solutionList.description: Docker on AWS EC2, RDS PostgreSQL, Upstash Redis with project key prefix, optional S3 media, Nginx TLS, Celery workers.

metricsList: 7 domain Django apps (users, organizations, events, locations, registrations, comments, notifications)
85+ HTTP operations including ViewSet actions and custom routes
Redis namespace social-events-api: for cache and Celery broker
JWT access 60 min / refresh 7 days with rotation and blacklist

mediaGallery.title: Social Events API — product views
mediaGallery.description: Screenshots and diagrams for portfolio presentation. Replace placeholder image URLs with Swagger UI, admin, or a frontend screenshot when available.

items.title: API cover
items.description: Social Events REST API for organizers and attendees

mediaItems.title: Cloud architecture
mediaItems.description: EC2 (Docker) → RDS PostgreSQL + Upstash Redis + optional S3

mediaItems.title: Local Docker stack
mediaItems.description: Postgres, Redis, API, Nginx, Certbot in docker-compose.local.yml

metrics.label: Django apps
metrics.description: users, organizations, events, locations, registrations, comments, notifications

layers.name: Presentation (clients)
layers.description: Web and mobile clients for attendees, organizers, and organization admins.

layers.name: API gateway & edge
layers.description: TLS termination and reverse proxy to Gunicorn on AWS EC2.
responsibilities: SSL termination and static file serving

layers.name: Application layer
layers.description: Django 5 + DRF monolith with domain apps and shared common package.

layers.name: Data & cache
layers.description: RDS PostgreSQL for transactional data; Upstash Redis for cache, rate limits, and Celery.
responsibilities: ACID persistence for events and registrations

layers.name: Async & integrations
layers.description: Background email tasks and OAuth providers.

designPatterns.title: App per bounded context
designPatterns.description: Each domain (events, registrations, comments) is a Django app with models, serializers, views, and optional signals.

designPatterns.title: Service layer (selective)
designPatterns.description: Notification and email logic lives in apps/notifications/services/; views stay thin with permission checks.

designPatterns.title: Custom permissions
designPatterns.description: Object-level permissions (IsEventHost, CanManageRegistrations) compose with organization membership.

designPatterns.title: Middleware cross-cutting
designPatterns.description: RateLimitMiddleware and AuditLogMiddleware wrap all requests before DRF.

designPatterns.title: Observer — signals
designPatterns.description: users, registrations, and comments apps register signals for future notification hooks.

designPatterns.title: Soft delete base model
designPatterns.description: common.models.BaseModel provides timestamps and soft-delete fields shared by domain models.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API containers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Run multiple Gunicorn workers per EC2 instance; add EC2 instances behind ALB as traffic grows.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Amazon RDS
scalabilityStrategies.description: Managed PostgreSQL with backups; scale instance class or add read replica for reporting (placeholder).

scalabilityStrategies.title: Upstash Redis
scalabilityStrategies.description: Offload rate-limit counters and cache; prefix keys when sharing instance across projects.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Celery workers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Decouple email and long tasks from HTTP threads; scale workers independently on EC2.

scalabilityStrategies.title: S3 for media
scalabilityStrategies.description: Avoid filling EC2 disk—serve uploads from S3 when USE_S3 is enabled.

securityStrategies.title: JWT + per-view permissions
securityStrategies.description: Default authentication is JWT; each viewset declares IsAuthenticated, IsEventHost, or AllowAny explicitly.

securityStrategies.title: Production TLS & HSTS
securityStrategies.description: SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT, secure cookies, HSTS, and SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER in production.py.

securityStrategies.title: Audit logging
securityStrategies.description: AuditLogMiddleware with sanitized bodies; sensitive fields redacted.

securityStrategies.title: Layered throttling
securityStrategies.description: Global middleware plus DRF AuthActionsThrottle, RegistrationThrottle, CheckInThrottle.

securityStrategies.title: OAuth via allauth
securityStrategies.description: Social tokens handled by django-allauth; API returns JWT via custom oauth views.

cacheStrategies.name: Django default cache
cacheStrategies.description: django-redis with KEY_PREFIX social-events-api: on Upstash (rediss://)
cacheStrategies.coverage: Rate limit windows, optional view cache

cacheStrategies.name: Celery broker & results
cacheStrategies.description: Redis DB 0 broker, DB 1 result backend; same global_keyprefix

cacheStrategies.name: Fail-open rate limits
cacheStrategies.description: Middleware allows traffic if Redis is down—documented tradeoff

architectureFeatures.title: Slug-based URLs
architectureFeatures.description: Events and organizations use slugs for readable public URLs.

architectureFeatures.title: Celery observability
architectureFeatures.description: GET /api/celery-health/ and task status endpoints for ops.

architectureFeatures.title: Docker profiles
architectureFeatures.description: Local full stack vs production web-only compose files.

requestFlow.title: Client request
requestFlow.description: Client sends HTTPS request with Bearer JWT for protected routes (events, registrations, profile).

requestFlow.title: Middleware
requestFlow.description: RateLimitMiddleware and AuditLogMiddleware run; DRF authenticates JWT and applies throttles.

requestFlow.title: View & permissions
requestFlow.description: ViewSet or APIView checks IsEventHost, CanManageRegistrations, etc., then validates serializer.

requestFlow.title: Persistence
requestFlow.description: ORM reads/writes RDS; cache keys updated in Upstash with social-events-api: prefix.

requestFlow.title: JSON response
requestFlow.description: DRF Response returns serializer data or standard error shape to client.

eventFlow.title: Domain action
eventFlow.description: User registers, books event, or comments—signal or view may trigger async work.

eventFlow.title: Celery enqueue
eventFlow.description: Task published to Redis broker DB 0 with prefixed keys.

eventFlow.title: Worker execution
eventFlow.description: Celery worker on EC2 runs users.send_verification_email or common.test_task.

eventFlow.title: Delivery
eventFlow.description: SMTP sends HTML email from apps/notifications/templates/; in-app Notification row created when wired.

decisions.title: Django monolith on EC2
decisions.problem: Need rapid feature delivery for event MVP without Kubernetes complexity.
decisions.solution: Single repo, Docker image, Gunicorn on EC2; domain split into Django apps.
decisions.outcome: Simple deploy path documented in docker/README.md; scale EC2/RDS vertically first.

decisions.title: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL
decisions.problem: Relational model for organizations, events, registrations, and foreign keys.
decisions.solution: RDS in production; SQLite only in config.settings.development for local runserver.
decisions.outcome: CONN_MAX_AGE 600 reduces connection overhead from Docker web container.

decisions.title: Upstash Redis with key prefix
decisions.problem: One Redis account shared across multiple portfolio projects on Upstash.
decisions.outcome: No key collisions; rediss:// for TLS to Upstash endpoint.

decisions.title: SimpleJWT + allauth
decisions.problem: SPA needs stateless auth; users want Google/GitHub sign-in.
decisions.solution: JWT for API; allauth stores social accounts; custom views return tokens.
decisions.outcome: Consistent Bearer flow; blacklist on logout when token_blacklist enabled.

decisions.title: drf-spectacular for API contract
decisions.problem: Frontend and portfolio need discoverable endpoint documentation.
decisions.outcome: Schema stays in sync with ViewSet decorators.

codeExamples.title: Redis key prefix for shared Upstash
codeExamples.description: Namespaces django-redis cache keys and Celery broker/result keys so multiple services can share one Upstash instance safely.

files.name: base.py
files.explanation: REDIS_KEY_PREFIX defaults to social-events-api: and applies to cache KEY_PREFIX and Celery global_keyprefix.

codeExamples.title: Event role & organization manager checks
codeExamples.description: Permission helpers combine per-event roles (host, moderator) with organization membership for cross-event administration.

files.name: permissions.py
files.explanation: Org owners/admins/managers can manage events linked to their organization even if not the original organizer.

codeExamples.title: Global rate limit middleware
codeExamples.description: IP-based limits for anonymous clients and user-id limits for authenticated users, backed by Django cache (Redis in production).

files.name: throttling.py
files.explanation: Fails open on Redis errors so cache outages do not hard-down the API.

codeExamples.title: Audit log with sensitive field redaction
codeExamples.description: Middleware logs request metadata and JSON bodies with passwords and tokens masked.

files.name: audit_log.py
files.explanation: SENSITIVE_FIELDS set prevents credential leakage into log aggregators on EC2.

codeExamples.title: Celery app with task lifecycle logging
codeExamples.description: Celery autodiscovers tasks from apps and common; signal handlers log task start, success, failure, and retry.

files.name: celery.py
files.explanation: Worker should run on EC2 alongside web container when email notifications must be reliable.
files.content:
app = Celery("social_events_api")
app.config_from_object("django.conf:settings", namespace="CELERY")
app.autodiscover_tasks()
app.autodiscover_tasks(["common"])
app.autodiscover_tasks(["apps"])

@task_prerun.connect
def task_prerun_handler(sender=None, task_id=None, task=None, **extra):
    logger.info(f"Task Started: {task.name} [ID: {task_id}]")

features.title: JWT authentication & social login
features.description: Email/password registration and login with SimpleJWT access/refresh tokens, rotation, blacklist on logout, plus Google and GitHub OAuth via django-allauth and dj-rest-auth.

features.title: Event lifecycle & discovery
features.description: Full event CRUD with draft/publish/cancel, public vs private events, categories, tags, recurrence rules, staff roles, favorites, and filtered list for discovery.
highlights: EventViewSet with slug lookup and publish/cancel actions
Role matrix: host, co-host, speaker, volunteer, moderator
ReadHeavyThrottle on list; WriteSensitiveThrottle on mutations

features.title: Organizations & memberships
features.description: Multi-tenant organizations with owner/admin/manager/member roles, invites, join/leave, and listing events owned by the organization.
highlights: Member invite and role update endpoints
Org managers can administer org-linked events via permissions

features.title: Ticket tiers & registrations
features.description: Per-event ticket tiers (price, capacity, visibility) and user registrations with status workflow, waitlist, cancellation, and host check-in.
highlights: Host-only registration list and status updates

features.title: Comments, likes & moderation
features.description: Threaded comments on events with author edit/delete, likes, and host/moderator pin/unpin for announcements.
highlights: Signals hook for future notification tasks

features.title: In-app notifications
features.description: User notification inbox with read/mark-all-read, stats, and delete; HTML email templates under apps/notifications/templates/.

features.title: Redis cache & Celery with key prefix
features.description: django-redis for cache and rate-limit counters; Celery broker and result backend on Redis with REDIS_KEY_PREFIX social-events-api: to avoid collisions on shared Upstash.
highlights: global_keyprefix on Celery broker and results
TLS rediss:// URL for Upstash in production .env

features.title: Layered rate limiting
features.description: Global RateLimitMiddleware (200/min anon IP, 1000/min auth user) plus DRF scoped throttles for auth, writes, registration, and check-in.

features.title: Request audit logging
features.description: AuditLogMiddleware records method, path, user, duration, and sanitized JSON body (passwords/tokens redacted) to the audit logger.
highlights: AuditLog model in common/models.py for domain audits

features.title: OpenAPI docs & cloud deploy
features.description: drf-spectacular schema and Swagger UI; Docker multi-stage image with Gunicorn; production compose runs web only against RDS and Upstash.
highlights: USE_S3 for media when enabled in production

metrics.label: Gunicorn bind
metrics.description: Inside web container; exposed via Nginx in local profile

metrics.label: Production host port
metrics.description: docker-compose.prod.yml maps host port to container 8000 on EC2

metrics.label: Local Postgres
metrics.description: Host port 5431 to avoid conflict with local PostgreSQL

metrics.label: Local Redis
metrics.description: Host port 6380; production uses Upstash rediss:// endpoint

metrics.label: DB pool
metrics.description: CONN_MAX_AGE on RDS in production settings

cloudServices.name: Amazon EC2
cloudServices.purpose: Runs Docker web container (social_events_api) with Gunicorn; Nginx on same host or separate instance for TLS termination (ports 80/443 local compose pattern)
cloudServices.cost: ~$15–40/mo (t3.small placeholder — update when instance type chosen)

cloudServices.name: Amazon RDS (PostgreSQL 15)
cloudServices.purpose: Managed primary database for all Django models (POSTGRES_HOST / DATABASE_URL in production .env)

cloudServices.name: Upstash Redis
cloudServices.purpose: TLS Redis (rediss://) for django-redis cache, rate limits, Celery broker (DB 0) and result backend (DB 1) with key prefix social-events-api:

cloudServices.name: Amazon S3
cloudServices.purpose: Optional media storage for avatars, event covers, org logos when USE_S3=true (django-storages + boto3)

cloudServices.name: SMTP / Amazon SES
cloudServices.purpose: Transactional email for registration, password reset, and notification templates

cloudServices.name: Google & GitHub OAuth
cloudServices.purpose: Social login providers configured via Django admin SocialApp or env (see docs/OAUTH_SETUP.md)

components.name: Web frontend (planned)
components.description: SPA or mobile app consuming /api/v2/ with JWT (placeholder URL in FRONTEND_URL)

components.name: API consumers
components.description: Third-party integrations via Bearer token and OpenAPI client

components.name: Swagger UI
components.description: Developers test at /api/docs/ on deployed host

components.name: Nginx reverse proxy
components.description: TLS, static/media volumes, proxy to Gunicorn (local compose; replicate on EC2)

components.name: Celery worker (recommended)
components.description: Separate process or container on EC2 for users.send_*_email and common.test_task

components.name: Certbot / ACM
components.description: Let's Encrypt in local compose; use ACM certificate on ALB in AWS (placeholder)

components.name: RDS PostgreSQL
components.description: social_events_db — users, events, registrations, comments, notifications

components.name: Upstash Redis
components.description: Prefixed keys; separate logical DB indexes 0 (broker) and 1 (cache/results)

dockerFiles.description: Full local stack: Postgres, Redis, web, Nginx, Certbot on social_events_network.
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    ports: ["5431:5432"]
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ["6380:6379"]
  web:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    environment:
      POSTGRES_HOST: postgres
      REDIS_HOST: redis
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379/1
      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0
  nginx:
    ports: ["80:80", "443:443"]

dockerFiles.description: Production profile: web container only; Postgres and Redis are external (RDS + Upstash).
dockerFiles.content:
services:
  web:
    build: { context: .., dockerfile: docker/dockerfile }
    env_file: ../.env
    environment:
      DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: config.settings.production
    ports: ["${PORT:-8000}:8000"]
    volumes: [logs_volume, static_volume, media_volume]
# Cloud: RDS + Upstash configured in ../.env

dockerFiles.description: Waits for REDIS_HOST/POSTGRES_HOST, migrates, optional superuser/populate, starts Celery detached, runs Gunicorn.
